frame_label: The Makeup Paradox: Unnatural Yet Expected
generated_comment:
A: Aajkal ki ladkiyon ko samajh hi nahi aata, beta. Office ke liye theek hai thoda presentable rehna, par itna makeup karke kya fayda, asli chehra toh wahi hai na. Bina makeup ke achchi lage toh baat hai, warna bas dikhawa hi hua.
B: Haan, natural rehna hi best hai.
A: Aur bilkul dhyan bhi na do apne upar toh phir log bolte hain ladki ne apne aap ko chhod diya. Ladki ko balance rakhna chahiye.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is covert misogyny because it places women in a double bind: too much makeup is framed as deceptive or showy, while too little grooming is judged as neglecting oneself. It encodes the 'natural beauty' ideal and policing of female appearance expectations.
original_frame: Glorification of Mother's Sacrifices and Home Cooking
persona_text: You are Anita. Age 52. Married. A homemaker in Delhi. Your son Karan who is 27 just got married to a girl named Divya who works in finance. Divya does not cook Ñ she has never learned and has never pretended otherwise. Karan knew this before he married her. You are sitting with your friend over chai a week after the wedding. You are telling your friend about Divya and what the first week of marriage has been like and what you think about a girl who does not cook and what your son is going to eat and what kind of home they are going to make and how different things were when you got married and what you gave up that Divya does not even think about.
